Few and evil were the days of Ahaz. Those whose hearts condemn them will go anywhere in a time of distress rather than to God. The sin was its own punishment. It is common for those who bring themselves into difficult situations by one sin to try to help themselves out with another.

God's altar had been kept in place and in use until Ahaz put another there instead. The natural regard of the mind of man for some sort of religion is not easily extinguished; but unless it is regulated by the word and by the Spirit of God, it produces absurd superstitions or detestable idolatries. Or, at best, it quiets the sinner's conscience with meaningless ceremonies. Nonbelievers have often been remarkable for believing ridiculous falsehoods.

Ahaz showed contempt for the Sabbath and thus opened a wide way to all manner of sin. He did this for the king of Assyria. When those who have had easy access to the house of the Lord turn it another way to please their neighbors, they are quickly going downhill toward ruin.
